{{Infobox character
| name = Silent King
| appears_in = Cradle
| titles = Dreadgod
| abilities = hunger madra, spiritual perception, parasitic technique, dream technique, technique to control the minds of the masses, mental assault, illusions
| affiliations = Silent Servants cult
| introduced = cradle-04-skysworn
}}

''See also: [[Silent King Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The Silent [[King]] is a [[Dreadgod]], an entity categorized as one of the most powerful beings in existence. Though often perceived by mortals as the weakest among its siblings, it is a formidable force capable of projecting illusions solid enough to engage [[Monarchs]] in combat. Physically, the entity is described as a relatively small white tiger with an oversized white halo, though it possesses a massive spiritual influence that can be felt for hundreds of miles. Its most lethal capability is a mental assault, which allows it to potentially touch the minds of dreamers across the entire planet. The Silent [[King]] maintains a vast kingdom within a jungle, where it manages a mental web that grants high intelligence and awareness to its subjects, including spirits, remnants, and various creatures. This domain is inhabited by the [[Silent Servants]], a cult known for being spies and liars who are skilled in brainwashing. The entity has also been associated with the [[Hollow King]] [[Path]] and possesses a powerful [[hunger aura]]. While it was once sealed away by [[Emriss Silentborn]], the Silent [[King]] was eventually killed by [[Wei Shi Lindon Arelius]].}}

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Silent King]] stirs in its dreams when it senses the [[Phoenix]] in battle.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c18">''Skysworn'', chapter 18</ref>}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Silent King]] is associated with the [[Silent Servants]] cult.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c10">''Uncrowned'', chapter 10</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Silent King]] is one of the most subtle and insidious of the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c6">''Wintersteel'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Silent King]] is a [[Dreadgod]] with no remaining representative in the [[Shen]] faction's audience tower.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c7">''Wintersteel'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Silent King]] is a suggested topic related to [[Emriss Silentborn]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c8">''Wintersteel'', chapter 8</ref>}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Silent King]] is theoretically the most vulnerable of the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c14">''Bloodline'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Silent King]] is a giant white tiger.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18">''Bloodline'', chapter 18</ref>}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The Silent [[King]] is a [[Dreadgod]] that resides within a purple-and-white tank. Measuring roughly the size of a human house, this entity possesses [[hunger madra]] and emits a [[hunger aura]]. Unlike its siblings, it has maintained its mental faculties and uses willpower to withstand its intense hunger urges. By utilizing spiritual perception, the creature monitors the boundaries of its kingdom while harboring a deep desire for revenge against [[Emriss Silentborn]]. Despite its power, an average human [[Herald]] is capable of overpowering the Silent [[King]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Silent King]] had a purple-and-white tank.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c19">''Reaper'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Silent King]] is a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24">''Reaper'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Silent King]] has maintained its mental faculties unlike its siblings.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Silent King]]'s body is roughly the size of a human house.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Silent King]] can be overpowered by an average human [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Silent King]] possesses [[hunger madra]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Silent King]] uses spiritual perception to monitor its kingdom's boundaries.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Silent King]] desires revenge against [[Emriss Silentborn]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Silent King]] can withstand its hunger urges through willpower.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Silent King]] experiences [[hunger aura]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24" />}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The Silent [[King]] is a small [[Dreadgod]], considered the weakest of his kind and a peer to the monster that destroyed the Valley, who was freed in central [[Everwood]]. Appearing as a white tiger with black stripes and smooth pale skin, this entity uses parasitic techniques and mental assaults to control people like helpless puppets. During a conflict where his dream technique washed over [[Moongrave]], he exerted influence by possessing members of [[Redmoon Hall]] and ordering the [[Golds]] to go wild in the city. Although [[Lindon]] crafted an [[Enforcer construct]] for him, the [[Dreadgod]] was eventually killed by the [[Void Sage]]. Following his death, [[Lindon]] sought to craft a [[Divine Treasure]] from the Silent [[King]]'s core binding, though the entity's power ultimately corrupted him.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is a [[Dreadgod]] who has been freed.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c2">''Dreadgod'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]]'s body is in hiding.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is located in central [[Everwood]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The situation regarding the [[Silent King]] is urgent.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is a character who refers to intruders as bold.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The people are puppets of the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is associated with horror stories.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] controls people like helpless puppets.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] threatened to attack [[Lindon]]'s friends and family.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] uses a parasitic technique.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c4">''Dreadgod'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is a threat to the [[Everwood continent]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] can speak.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5">''Dreadgod'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] has a technique.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is a peer to the monster that destroyed the Valley.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] made an [[Enforcer construct]] for the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c7">''Dreadgod'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is an entity that people bow before.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c14">''Dreadgod'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] has white crowns.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] whispered into [[Lindon]]'s mind that he keeps his promises.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]]'s dream technique washed over [[Moongrave]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c16">''Dreadgod'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]]'s will and techniques are present in the battle.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] has a halo.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] can use a technique to control the minds of the masses.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] ordered the [[Golds]] to go wild in the city.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] can exert greater control over [[Remnants]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17">''Dreadgod'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] can possess members of [[Redmoon Hall]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]]'s influence can bolster the willpower of those he possesses.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] had thralls that tried to attack [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] calls the [[Dreadgod]]'s halo the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is a white tiger with smooth pale skin and black stripes.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18">''Dreadgod'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]]'s face resembles a detailed [[Remnant]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]]'s crown shines bright as the sun.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is a [[Dreadgod]] who appears as a giant tiger.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The Silent [[King's army]] is fighting in the distance.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King's madra]] can be identified by eating it.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]]'s deadliest power is its mental assault.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is a small [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is a figure whose victory [[Lindon]] sees in a dream.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is considered the weakest of the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] was on the verge of death.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c19">''Dreadgod'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King’s madra]] should be useful in divining the future.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21">''Dreadgod'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is a [[Dreadgod]] that [[Lindon]] killed.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The illusions used were the illusions of the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c22">''Dreadgod'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] possesses [[madra]] that can fuel illusions.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] wants to craft a [[Divine Treasure]] from the [[Silent King]]’s core binding.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] is gone, having been killed by the [[Void Sage]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]] has a specific technique that involves illusory clones.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c23">''Dreadgod'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Silent King]]'s power corrupted [[Wei Shi Lindon Arelius]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24">''Dreadgod'', chapter 24</ref>}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|After being consumed by [[Lindon]], the Silent [[King]]'s spirit—composed of bones, tendons, and various other materials—was repurposed to create special arrows and purple orbs. The entity's [[madra]], which produces white light, was used to stimulate [[Lindon]]'s [[Dreadgod arm]] and allowed for the projection of illusions solid enough to trade blows with [[Monarchs]]. Following the Silent [[King]]'s death, which empowered the [[Weeping Dragon]] and resulted in a [[binding]] more similar to a normal one, the entity was forced to swap places with the [[Wandering Titan]]. The Silent [[King]] remains associated with a halo that serves as a [[Goldsign]] for the [[Silent Servants]], and the scale of [[Dross]]'s [[dream aura]] once temporarily matched its own.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] consumed the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c3">''Waybound'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Silent King]]'s spirit consists of bones, tendons, and other material.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c4">''Waybound'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Silent King]]'s body material is used to make [[Lindon]]'s special arrows.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c5">''Waybound'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]]'s [[Dreadgod arm]] is stimulated by the [[Silent King]]’s modified [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c7">''Waybound'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] uses a [[Silent King]] illusion.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c10">''Waybound'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Silent King]] has [[madra]] that produces white light.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Silent King]] can project illusions solid enough to trade blows with [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Weeping Dragon]] was empowered by the death of the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c14">''Waybound'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Silent King]] has died.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c18">''Waybound'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] used materials from the [[Silent King]] to create purple orbs.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c20">''Waybound'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Silent King]] was forced to swap places with the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c21">''Waybound'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Weeping Dragon]]'s [[binding]] is more like a normal [[binding]] than the [[Silent King]]’s.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Silent King]] is associated with a halo that serves as a [[Goldsign]] for the [[Silent Servants]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c22">''Waybound'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The scale of [[Dross]]'s [[dream aura]] temporarily matched the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c27">''Waybound'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Silent King]] challenged the wards of [[House Arelius]] and was defeated.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c1">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Dross]] wears the pure-white halo of the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kahn Mala]] was the former cult leader of the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Silent King]] was traded places with by the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 14</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|“The Silent King is theoretically the most vulnerable of the Dreadgods, yet we were no more successful in damaging it there than anywhere else.”<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c14" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|“The Silent King’s body is in hiding,” Emriss continued,<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c2" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The Silent King’s voice from the door was amused. “Intruders. How bold.”<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|“Lindon,” the Silent King whispered into his mind, “I keep my promises.”<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|“The Silent King can exert greater control over Remnants,” the Sage said.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
